STt^rcjJEFTTU •ffl.^T.f^. 879(3?) t%W 5-12-2001 MINISTRY OF EXTERNAL AFFAIRS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 28th March. 2002 G.S.R. 235(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred b\ section 24, read with section 5 of the Passports Act. 1967 (15 of 1967), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules to further amend the Passports Rules, 1980, namely — „ 1 (1) These rules may be called the Passports (Amendment)) Rules, 2002 (2) They shall come into force with effect from the 1 st day of April, 2002 2 In Schedule IV to the Passports Rules. 1980.— (a) for serial numbers 1 to 28 and the entries relating thereto, the following shall be substituted, namely — SCHEDULE IV (.See rule 8) SCHEDULE OF FEES PAYABLE IN RESPECT OF APPLICATIONS FOR PASSPORT AND TRAVEL DOCUMENTS SI No 1 Particulars of application 2 NEW PASSPORTS— 1 Ordinary passport booklet containing 36 pages with a maximum \ ahdity often years (no extra fee ^ ill be charged for renewals upto ten > ears if initial vahdit\ is less) For issuing passport booklet containing sixt) pages India Rs -•> 1000 1500 Scale of fees $ 4 40 50 Abioad Euro (For countries \\ ith Euro as the currency) 5 48 60 -- 4 of 8 -- 5 1 2 3 2 Ordinary passport containing 36 pages with a maximum validity of five years for minor children below the age of 18 years India-Bangladesh or India-Sn Lanka Passport with a maximum validity of ten years SPECIAL TRAVEL DOCUMENT— 4 5 6 Emergency Certificate Certificate of identity with a maximum validity often years Additional ordinary passport booklet containing 36 pages for any country under the proviso to rule 13 upto initial validity of one year (no extra fee w ill be charged for renewals upto ten years) RENEWAL OF TRAVEL DOCUMENTS— 7 8 Renewal of India-Bangladesh or India-Sn Lanka passport with a maximum total validity often years after initial validity of three/four years (the renewal fee will be charged in one lump sum once only, even though period of validity may be less than three/four years, depending upon discretion of Passport Issuing Authority or according to instructions from Government authorities) Renewal of Emergency Certificate or Certificate of Identity MISCELLANEOUS SERVICES— 9 10 11 12 13 Additional endorsement or other miscellaneous service on ordinary passport (not being a miscellaneous service referred to in entry ten of this Schedule) Miscellaneous services (including additional booket) requiring issue of a fresh ordinary passport booklet containing 36 pages For issuing passport booklet containing 60 pages Additional endorsement or miscellaneous service on an Emergency Certificate or Certificate of Identity Miscellaneous service on an India-Bangladesh or India-Sn Lanka Passport An> miscellaneous services (including additional booklet) requiring issue of a fresh India-Bangladesh passport or an India-Sn Lanka passport DUPLICATE PASSPORTS AND OTHER DOUCMENTS IN LIEU OF LOSTORDAMAGED DOCUMENTS— 14 15 Issue of duplicate passport containing 36 pages m lieu of the ordinary' passport on the ground that it has been lost stolen damaged, destroyed or for am other such reasons For issuing duplicate passport booklet containing 60 pages Issue of duplicate Emergency Certificate or Certificate of Identity m lieu of Emergency Certificate/Certificate of Identity on the ground tiiat it has been lost, stolen, damaged or destroyed or for any other such reasons 3 600 3)0 — 300 1000 300 150 ' 500 700 100 100 500 2500 300 4 25 20 5 12 40 10 5 20 25 5 5 20 125 140 25 5 30 25 6 15 48 12 6 25 30 6 6 25 150 165 30 -- 5 of 8 -- 6 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA .EXTRAORDINARY |PARTII—SEC 3(i)] 1 16. 2 Issue of duplicate passport in lieu of the India- Bangladesh or India-Sri Lanka Passport on the ground that it has been lost, stolen, damaged or destroyed or for any other such reasons, (b) Serial numbers 29 to 35 shall be renumbered as serial numbers 17 to 23 thereof. 3 1000 4 30 5 36 fF. No W401/66/2000] S. R TAYAL, Jt. Secy.
